summary:
Glanzmann disease is a congenital defect of platelets causing impaired platelet aggregation due to defective glycoprotein IIb/IIIa receptors. It is distinct from clotting factor deficiencies and affects intrinsically abnormal platelet function rather than deficient plasma proteins.  

$\boxed{A}$

It is distinct from clotting factor deficiencies and affects intrinsically abnormal platelet function rather than deficient plasma proteins.  

$\boxed{A}$. Answer choice: A
